limitations: Private repositories, employer work, code review outside GitHub, and design work are not inferred.
Raw commit counts, contribution streaks, follower counts, and language percentages are not primary scores.
Forked and archived repositories are excluded by default.
Only the strongest 8 eligible repositories are summarized in this card.

gaps.whyItMatters: Maintenance signals are easier to trust when tests, automation, and change history are visible.
Completeness signals help other people understand, run, and reuse the project.
Shipping signals show that the repository can be used beyond source browsing.
Collaboration signals make it easier for outsiders to participate safely and predictably.

limitations: These are improvement hints, not a developer ranking.
Private work and non-GitHub maintenance are not inferred.
